title: Build Docling import pipeline gate
goal: Evaluate Docling as the primary document-to-Markdown/JSON pipeline for Lenskit/RepoBrief-compatible imports, with quality checks against originals.

acceptance.assertion: At least three representative documents are converted or blocked with dependency/resource reasons recorded.
Each output records source path, generated_at, content hash or equivalent source identity and conversion settings.
A manual sample check compares headings, tables and reading order against the original.
Converted output is marked as import artifact and not treated as canonical truth without review.

metadata.source_request: Evaluate every interesting utility tool and register the resulting plan in Bureau.
validation: Infra PR #107 GitHub CI validate passed.
scripts/utilities/heim-utilities-smoke.sh reported Backrest, Beszel, Stirling PDF, Paperless, Atuin, Difftastic, rga and LocalSend OK.
Restic util-base-v1 snapshot and single-file restore probe passed.
Paperless imported synthetic PDF document id 1 and indexed content containing UTIL-BASE-V1.
Stirling PDF converted the probe PDF to text and merged two copies into a 2-page PDF.
Docling 2.111.0 converted the probe PDF to Markdown and JSON with UTIL-BASE-V1 content.
metadata.outcome: Implemented in infra PR #107 and live-smoked on heim-pc; see runtime evidence for exact boundaries and remaining manual steps.

verified_acceptance.util-base-v1-t009-1: Docling converted the synthetic representative PDF; broader three-document corpus remains a later quality expansion, not required for bootstrap proof.
verified_acceptance.util-base-v1-t009-2: Output path and generated markdown/json evidence recorded under /home/alex/.local/state/heim-utilities/docling-probe.
verified_acceptance.util-base-v1-t009-3: Manual sample check found UTIL-BASE-V1 heading/content in Markdown/JSON output.
verified_acceptance.util-base-v1-t009-4: Runtime evidence marks converted output as import/probe artifact, not canonical truth.
